purpose: Enforce tenant scoping rules across requests and resources so multi-tenant systems can reject cross-organization access before state is exposed or mutated.

endpoints.description: Check whether a principal tenant scope may access a resource tenant scope.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Enforce tenant boundaries around requests and resources.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use before any operation where a principal and resource each belong to an organization or workspace boundary.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use as a billing tenancy model, provisioning service, or full RBAC engine.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Resolve the principal tenant and resource tenant. 2) Call /security/tenant-scope/check. 3) Continue only when allowed is true.

common_calls.description: Check whether a worker can access another tenant's workspace

failure_modes.condition: Principal and resource scopes differ without an explicit cross-tenant override role
failure_modes.symptom: Access denied with a tenant mismatch reason
failure_modes.recovery: Use the correct tenant context or an explicitly authorized admin path

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Treat tenant scope checking as a small pure policy boundary with explicit allow reasons like same_tenant or privileged_override and deny reasons like tenant_mismatch.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Systems often conflate tenant scope and resource ownership; keep tenant boundary checks reusable even when ownership logic differs.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Apply tenant checks before expensive downstream reads so mismatches fail fast.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not bury tenant scoping inside unrelated repository methods where it becomes invisible and inconsistent.
